sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking on the orange object at (12,24).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current objects (pixels > 5):")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  ({cr},{cc}) color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check gray-bordered object children
print("\nGray-bordered object children:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        cr = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        cc = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        for child_id in n['children']:
            child = seg['nodes'][child_id]
            print(f"  G at ({cr},{cc}) child: color={child['color']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
After clicking on the orange object at (12,24):
- White cursor moved from (6,17) to (12,24)
- Orange object moved from (12,24) to (14,27) - it moved down the trail
- Purple object stayed at (41,49)
- Gray-bordered object children unchanged: O, O, p, p
So clicking on the orange object moves the orange object down the trail, and the white cursor follows.
Now I understand better:
- Clicking on the orange object moves the orange object down the trail.
- Clicking on the purple circle (which is the white cursor's initial position) moves the purple object up the trail.
- The white cursor always follows the object that was clicked on.
Let me think about the goal. The gray-bordered objects have interior colors O, O, p, p. Maybe the goal is to move the orange object through the purple-interior gray-bordered objects to change them to orange, or vice versa.
Looking at the trail, the order seems to be:
White cursor -> Orange -> ... -> Purple -> ...
Let me try clicking on the orange object again to move it further down the trail.
